The Call of the Wild (2020)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $62,342,368Details
International Box Office $45,008,823
Worldwide Box Office $107,351,191
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $7,100,475
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $9,962,726
Total $17,063,201
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Buck is a big-hearted dog whose blissful domestic life is turned upside down when he is suddenly uprooted from his California home and transplanted to the exotic wilds of the Alaskan Yukon during the Gold Rush of the 1890s. As the newest rookie on a mail delivery dog sled team—and later its leader—Buck experiences the adventure of a lifetime, ultimately finding his true place in the world and becoming his own master.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$24,791,624 (39.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.51 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:58.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$125,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,752 opening theaters/3,914 max. theaters, 3.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $81,221,652

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 21st, 2020 (Wide) by 20th Century Studios
International Releases: January 3rd, 2020 (Wide) (Poland)
February 20th, 2020 (Wide), released as ЗОВ ПРЕДКОВ (Russia (CIS))
February 20th, 2020 (Wide), released as Il richiamo della foresta (Italy)
February 20th, 2020 (Wide) (France)
February 20th, 2020 (Wide) (Germany)
Video Release: March 27th, 2020 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some violence, peril, thematic elements and mild language.
(Rating bulletin 2612 (Cert #52529), 1/15/2020)
Running Time: 100 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead
Geographical setting: Arctic Circle, Canada, Yukon
Plot point: Autumn Years
Social setting: Gold Rush
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Target audience: Family Movie
Time period setting: 1890s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: 20th Century Studios, TSG Entertainment, 3 Arts Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

As the newest rookie on a mail delivery dog sled team—and later its leader—Buck experiences the adventure of a lifetime, ultimately finding his true place in the world and becoming his own master.
